animeApocalypseCodes.title: Anime Apocalypse Codes
animeApocalypseCodes.subtitle: Redeem active Anime Apocalypse codes for coins, ability spins, gadget spins, boost timers, raid tickets, and trait rerolls. Use these rewards before heavy early-game rolling so your first runs start with stronger abilities and gadgets.
animeApocalypseCodes.intro: This Anime Apocalypse codes module is built for players who want current reward codes and a clear redemption flow. Active codes are ordered first, followed by expired codes that players commonly search for after release and testing milestones.
animeApocalypseCodes.redeemTitle: How to Redeem Anime Apocalypse Codes
redeemSteps: Click the Codes icon on the right side of the screen.
Paste the code into the text box.
Press Confirm to claim the rewards.

items.bestUse: Claim this Anime Apocalypse code on a new or private server before spending early spins so the release milestone rewards can support your first ability and gadget rolls.

animeApocalypseBeginnerGuide.title: Anime Apocalypse Beginner Guide
animeApocalypseBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Start from the lobby, clear zombie waves, choose useful cards, and spend your first rewards on the systems that matter most. This Anime Apocalypse guide turns the first few runs into a simple progression path.
animeApocalypseBeginnerGuide.intro: Anime Apocalypse is a Roblox action RPG where players fight zombie waves across anime-inspired maps, earn loot, roll for abilities and gadgets, and strengthen runs with cards, traits, pets, cosmetics, and upgrades. The best early path is to learn movement, clear accessible rooms, use codes for spins, and build around cooldown speed, economy, and consistent wave clear.

items.title: Spawn in the Anime Apocalypse lobby and open the play area
items.goal: Understand the basic Anime Apocalypse flow before spending spins or currency.
items.details: You begin in the Anime Apocalypse lobby with a starting ability, then enter the play area to select a map and begin clearing zombie-filled levels.
actions: Pick an early map instead of jumping straight into hard content.
Start a run to learn the room flow and enemy pacing.
items.beginnerTip: Use the first Anime Apocalypse run to learn where enemies spawn and how exits open after rooms are cleared.

items.title: Learn the Anime Apocalypse core controls
items.goal: Make Anime Apocalypse combat and movement feel automatic before harder waves.
items.details: Basic attack is handled with LMB, active skills are mapped to Z, X, C, and V, gadgets use E, ultimate uses G, dash uses Q, and slide uses Ctrl.
actions: Chain double jump and air dash for repositioning.
Use skills in rotation instead of saving every cooldown for bosses.
items.beginnerTip: Fast movement is a major Anime Apocalypse advantage because many rooms reward quick repositioning and clean wave clearing.

items.title: Choose an Anime Apocalypse beginner-friendly mode and map
items.goal: Clear Anime Apocalypse runs consistently before chasing harder rewards.
items.details: Survival-style dungeon runs are the main early learning path, while Infinite mode becomes better once you understand movement, cards, and cooldown cycles. Shibuya is the easier launch map, while Impel Down adds more demanding room layouts and visual hazards.
actions: Start with Shibuya for cleaner room flow.
Move to Impel Down after you are comfortable with dash and wall movement.
Use Infinite mode when you want wave practice and long-session farming.
items.beginnerTip: Do not panic around lava-style sections in Anime Apocalypse Impel Down; some sections are traversable and are part of the map flow.

items.title: Clear Anime Apocalypse waves and pick the right cards
items.goal: Turn each Anime Apocalypse run into a stronger build instead of relying only on your base ability.
items.details: After boss or wave milestones, Anime Apocalypse can offer cards that change a run with bonuses such as wealth, extra lives, cooldown reduction, and drop chance boosts.
actions: Pick Wealth early when you need more economy.
Shift into cooldown reduction once your currency flow is stable.
Use extra lives for safety in longer runs and Infinite mode.
items.beginnerTip: Cooldown cards become more valuable once your Anime Apocalypse ability rotation is strong enough to clear rooms quickly.

items.title: Spend early Anime Apocalypse rewards on spins and upgrades
items.goal: Improve your Anime Apocalypse build before trying to brute-force harder content.
items.details: Coins, spins, gadgets, traits, pets, and cosmetics all feed into progression, but early players should focus on ability and gadget access plus upgrades that help room clearing.
actions: Redeem active Anime Apocalypse codes before heavy rolling.
Use Normal and Lucky Ability Spins to expand your ability options.
Prioritize cooldown-speed upgrades, then movement and economy support.
items.beginnerTip: Cooldown speed is a high-priority Anime Apocalypse upgrade because faster skill cycling improves both wave clear and boss pressure.

items.title: Build around one reliable Anime Apocalypse ability first
items.goal: Avoid wasting Anime Apocalypse resources by constantly swapping before your first stable build is online.
items.details: Abilities are the primary combat system, while gadgets add a separate active tool. Early players should keep a consistent ability long enough to learn its range, cooldowns, damage pattern, ultimate timing, and awakening timing.
actions: Use Z, X, C, and V as your main rotation.
Save G for high-value ultimate moments.
Pair crowd-control gadgets with abilities that need grouped enemies.
items.beginnerTip: A consistent Anime Apocalypse ability with clean cooldown usage is stronger than a rare pull that you do not understand yet.

animeApocalypseAbilityTierList.title: Anime Apocalypse Ability Tier List
animeApocalypseAbilityTierList.subtitle: Compare the best Anime Apocalypse abilities for early farming, boss rooms, Infinite mode, and late-game clearing. Rankings focus on performance, summon value, mobility, cooldown efficiency, and how practical each ability is to use.
animeApocalypseAbilityTierList.intro: Abilities are the main combat tool in Anime Apocalypse, and every ability uses a skill kit built around active skills, ultimate timing, and awakening value. The strongest early choices are consistent wave clearers, while the strongest long-term chase is the rare Supreme-tier option.

abilities.name: Saiyan
abilities.rankingReason: Saiyan is the rarest Supreme-tier Anime Apocalypse ability and has the strongest raw performance, combining major walk-speed value with high damage output.
abilities.tradeoff: The 0.1% Supreme pull rate makes Saiyan a long-term Anime Apocalypse target rather than the ability most players should plan around on day one.

abilities.name: Shadow Monarch
abilities.rankingReason: Shadow Monarch is a popular Anime Apocalypse early choice because its Arise-style summon mechanic creates pressure while the player moves through zombie waves.
abilities.tradeoff: Shadow Monarch gets the most Anime Apocalypse value when you keep enemies under control and let summons contribute throughout the room.

abilities.name: Coyote
abilities.rankingReason: Coyote is one of the most popular Anime Apocalypse early choices thanks to consistent damage output, wolf-type summons, and smooth move animations.
abilities.tradeoff: Coyote rewards active Anime Apocalypse movement and clean skill rotation more than passive play.

abilities.name: Stark
abilities.rankingReason: Stark brings a gun-type Anime Apocalypse moveset with a strong area-of-effect ultimate, making it valuable when enemies are grouped or when a room needs burst damage.
abilities.tradeoff: Stark gets its best Anime Apocalypse value from timing the ultimate into dense enemy packs instead of using it on scattered targets.

abilities.name: King of Curses
abilities.rankingReason: King of Curses belongs to the Anime Apocalypse ability roster and works as a strong main-combat option when players want a full offensive kit before chasing the rarest pulls.
abilities.tradeoff: King of Curses performance depends on learning the full Anime Apocalypse skill sequence, ultimate timing, and awakening usage.

abilities.name: Sosuke
abilities.rankingReason: Sosuke is part of the Anime Apocalypse gacha ability pool and fits players who want a primary ability for regular runs while building toward higher-value chase options.
abilities.tradeoff: Sosuke should be evaluated by Anime Apocalypse cooldown flow and room clear consistency rather than name value alone.

abilities.name: Nen Prodigy
abilities.rankingReason: Nen Prodigy gives Anime Apocalypse players another listed ability route for building a stable kit before committing premium spins to rarer targets.
abilities.tradeoff: Nen Prodigy is best used as a learned Anime Apocalypse main ability, not as a temporary roll that gets replaced before mastery develops.

abilities.name: Zorro
abilities.rankingReason: Zorro is the default Anime Apocalypse starter weapon available to players, making it the natural first option for learning movement, targeting, and skill timing.
abilities.tradeoff: Move on once you roll a stronger, smoother, or more specialized Anime Apocalypse ability.

abilities.name: Fire Fighter
abilities.rankingReason: Fire Fighter is tied to Anime Apocalypse Battle Pass progression and has a flashy ultimate, but it clears dungeons more slowly than stronger practical picks.
abilities.tradeoff: Use Fire Fighter when you want the Anime Apocalypse Battle Pass weapon experience, not when you need the fastest clear speed.

animeApocalypseAbilitiesGuide.title: Anime Apocalypse Abilities Guide
animeApocalypseAbilitiesGuide.subtitle: Learn how Anime Apocalypse abilities work, how to roll them, how Normal and Lucky Spins differ, and which known abilities are currently part of the launch roster. Use this before spending code rewards or premium spin bundles.
animeApocalypseAbilitiesGuide.intro: Abilities are the primary way to fight zombie waves in Anime Apocalypse. Each ability has three regular skills plus an Ultimate skill and an Awakening skill, while the Ability Gacha uses Normal Spins and Lucky Spins across Rare, Epic, Legendary, Mythic, and Supreme rarity categories.

items.name: Anime Apocalypse Ability Skill Structure
items.details: Each Anime Apocalypse ability is built around three regular skills, an Ultimate skill, and an Awakening skill.
items.playerUse: Rotate regular skills for Anime Apocalypse wave clearing, save the Ultimate for boss rooms or dense enemy packs, and use Awakening when the fight needs a power spike.

items.name: Anime Apocalypse Ability Gacha
items.details: Anime Apocalypse abilities are obtained by opening the Ability Gacha menu with E and rolling with Normal Spins or Lucky Spins.
controls: Lobby Ability Gacha: press E to open the rolling menu
items.playerUse: Use Normal Spins for broad Anime Apocalypse early rolls and Lucky Spins when you want a better chance at higher-rarity abilities.

items.name: Anime Apocalypse Rarity Rates
items.details: Anime Apocalypse ability rarities are listed as Rare 62.1%, Epic 32%, Legendary 5%, Mythic 0.8%, and Supreme 0.1%.
items.playerUse: Treat Supreme abilities as long-term Anime Apocalypse chase pulls and build a stable run around the best ability you already own.

items.name: Anime Apocalypse Pity Rules
items.details: The Anime Apocalypse Ability Gacha has pity milestones: 50 rolls guarantee a Legendary ability and 100 rolls guarantee a Mythic ability.
items.playerUse: Do not burn every Anime Apocalypse Lucky Spin immediately; plan bigger rolling sessions when your pity count and code rewards can work together.

items.name: Zorro
items.obtainMethod: Available as the Anime Apocalypse starter weapon for all players
items.details: Zorro is the practical first Anime Apocalypse ability for learning attacks, room movement, and basic skill rotation.
items.playerUse: Use Zorro for the first few Anime Apocalypse runs, then compare new rolls against its clear speed and comfort.

items.name: Nen Prodigy
items.details: Nen Prodigy is part of the known Anime Apocalypse ability roster and can be used as a main combat kit while progressing through early and mid-game rooms.
items.playerUse: Keep Nen Prodigy long enough to learn its Anime Apocalypse rotation before replacing it with a higher-performing pull.

items.name: Sosuke
items.details: Sosuke is one of the listed abilities in the Anime Apocalypse roster and works as a main ability option for regular clearing.
items.playerUse: Evaluate Sosuke by Anime Apocalypse cooldown flow, range comfort, and how well it clears grouped zombie waves.

items.name: King of Curses
items.details: King of Curses is a listed Anime Apocalypse ability option for players building around a stronger offensive kit.
items.playerUse: Use King of Curses as a main damage ability and learn where its Ultimate and Awakening give the highest value.

items.name: Coyote
items.details: Coyote uses wolf-type summons and smooth move animations, making it one of the most popular practical choices for Anime Apocalypse early progression.
items.playerUse: Pick Coyote when you want consistent Anime Apocalypse wave damage with strong mobility-friendly gameplay.

items.name: Shadow Monarch
items.details: Shadow Monarch is known for an Arise-style mechanic that summons wolf allies and provides consistent pressure during Anime Apocalypse zombie waves.
items.playerUse: Use Shadow Monarch for Anime Apocalypse early farming and sustained room clearing while you move aggressively around enemy groups.

items.name: Stark
items.details: Stark has a gun-type Anime Apocalypse moveset and a strong area-of-effect ultimate for burst damage.
items.playerUse: Save Stark ultimate for dense groups, boss pressure windows, or rooms where enemies can be pulled together by a gadget.

items.name: Saiyan
items.details: Saiyan is the rarest Supreme-tier Anime Apocalypse ability and the strongest raw-performance chase option, with major walk-speed and damage value.
items.playerUse: Treat Saiyan as a long-term Anime Apocalypse goal and avoid delaying progression while waiting for a 0.1% pull.

items.name: Fire Fighter
items.obtainMethod: Anime Apocalypse Battle Pass final-level reward
items.details: Fire Fighter is the Anime Apocalypse Battle Pass weapon and has a flashy ultimate, but its dungeon clear speed is slower than the strongest practical picks.
items.playerUse: Use Fire Fighter for Anime Apocalypse collection, Battle Pass completion value, and variety rather than maximum farming speed.

animeApocalypseGadgetsGuide.title: Anime Apocalypse Gadgets Guide for Safer Wave Control
animeApocalypseGadgetsGuide.subtitle: Use Anime Apocalypse gadgets as secondary active skills that support your main ability loadout with crowd control, defense, enemy grouping, and safer wave clearing.
animeApocalypseGadgetsGuide.intro: Anime Apocalypse gadgets are separate from your main anime-inspired ability and give each run another tool for handling zombie pressure. Normal Gadget Spins and Lucky Gadget Spins help unlock gadget options, while strong picks such as Black Hole and Infinity can define how safely and quickly you clear dungeon rooms or survive Infinite mode.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Gadget Slot
items.description: The gadget slot acts as a secondary active skill that helps your ability handle zombie waves. A good gadget adds offense, defense, crowd control, or emergency protection depending on its effect.
items.bestFor: Players who want extra safety and faster wave control
notes: Runs alongside your main Anime Apocalypse ability
Supports both dungeon clearing and Infinite mode
Works best when picked to solve a clear weakness in your build

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Normal Gadget Spins
items.description: Normal Gadget Spins roll the gadget system through the same gacha-style progression loop used by other Anime Apocalypse upgrades. They are useful early because code rewards often help players build a starter gadget pool quickly.
items.bestFor: Early gadget collection and replacing weak starter rolls
notes: Good for building a first usable gadget pool
Commonly paired with code reward planning
Best spent before using rarer gadget resources

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Lucky Gadget Spins
items.description: Lucky Gadget Spins are more valuable because they support higher-rarity gadget hunting. Save them for sessions where you are actively trying to improve your gadget slot instead of casually rerolling between short runs.
items.bestFor: Chasing stronger gadgets such as Black Hole or Infinity
notes: Appears in major Anime Apocalypse release rewards
Best saved for focused reroll sessions
Most useful once you know your preferred playstyle

items.title: Black Hole
items.description: Black Hole pulls nearby enemies together, making it one of the most practical Anime Apocalypse gadgets for clearing dense zombie groups. It shines when your main ability has area damage or when dungeon rooms become crowded.
items.bestFor: Dungeon clearing, wave grouping, and farming speed
notes: Pairs well with fast cooldown rotations

items.title: Infinity
items.description: Infinity creates a protective bubble that stops enemies from reaching you for a short duration. It is especially strong in Anime Apocalypse Infinite mode because heavy enemy pressure makes defensive uptime valuable.
items.bestFor: Infinite mode, survival, and emergency defense
notes: Helps when enemies flood the screen
Combines well with extra-life and survival card choices

items.title: Time Stop
items.description: Time Stop gives a brief control window, but Anime Apocalypse rewards fast movement, repositioning, and quick wave clearing. It can still help defensive players, especially while learning enemy pacing.
items.bestFor: Situational control and slower defensive play
notes: Less efficient than Black Hole for fast clears
Better as a temporary control option than a final farming target

animeApocalypseGadgetTierList.title: Anime Apocalypse Gadget Tier List for Dungeon and Infinite Mode
animeApocalypseGadgetTierList.subtitle: Compare Anime Apocalypse gadgets by crowd control, defense, Infinite mode value, boss-room usefulness, and farming speed.
animeApocalypseGadgetTierList.intro: Gadgets change how an Anime Apocalypse run feels because they add a separate active tool on top of your main ability. Black Hole and Infinity are the strongest practical choices for crowd-heavy gameplay, while Time Stop is more situational because the game rewards fast movement and rapid room clearing.

items.name: Infinity
strengths: Very strong when enemies fill the screen
Excellent for Anime Apocalypse Infinite mode
items.keepOrReroll: Keep Infinity and build Anime Apocalypse survival runs around its defensive window.

items.name: Black Hole
items.keepOrReroll: Keep Black Hole, especially when your Anime Apocalypse ability has strong area damage.

items.name: High-control gadget slot
strengths: Useful when it groups enemies, prevents damage, or stabilizes boss rooms
Worth testing before spending rare rerolls
items.keepOrReroll: Keep temporarily if it clearly improves Anime Apocalypse safety or clear speed.

items.name: Time Stop
strengths: Can create a brief control window
items.keepOrReroll: Reroll Time Stop when you are chasing faster Anime Apocalypse farming or stronger Infinite mode survival.

items.name: Low-impact gadget rolls
strengths: Can help before you obtain a stronger gadget
Acceptable while saving Lucky Gadget Spins
items.keepOrReroll: Replace low-impact rolls once you can target a stronger Anime Apocalypse crowd-control or defensive gadget.

animeApocalypseTraitsGuide.title: Anime Apocalypse Traits Guide: When to Reroll and What to Keep
animeApocalypseTraitsGuide.subtitle: Traits are passive modifiers rolled onto Anime Apocalypse abilities to improve damage, movement, cooldown flow, or survivability.
animeApocalypseTraitsGuide.intro: Anime Apocalypse Trait Rerolls are valuable progression resources, so the best approach is to spend them on abilities you plan to keep. Strong traits matter most when attached to high-rarity or long-term abilities that already fit your farming, boss-room, or Infinite mode plan.

rows.whatItImproves: High-end offensive value for stronger Anime Apocalypse ability builds
rows.bestUseCase: Players building around a strong ability and looking for better damage performance
rows.rerollAdvice: Keep Reaper on a strong Anime Apocalypse ability and avoid replacing it casually.

rows.bestUseCase: Fast dungeon clears, mobile playstyles, and safer movement through crowded rooms
rows.rerollAdvice: Keep Flash if your Anime Apocalypse build benefits from speed and room-to-room tempo.

rows.whatItImproves: Wave clearing, boss damage, and farming speed
rows.bestUseCase: Main abilities used for dungeon grinding or high-pressure combat
rows.rerollAdvice: Spend rerolls here when the Anime Apocalypse ability is rare enough to keep long term.

rows.whatItImproves: Skill rotation speed and ability uptime
rows.bestUseCase: Abilities that rely on cycling multiple skills quickly
rows.rerollAdvice: Target cooldown value once your Anime Apocalypse economy and ability choice are stable.

rows.whatItImproves: Dodging, repositioning, and faster map flow
rows.bestUseCase: Infinite mode survival and speed-focused dungeon clears
rows.rerollAdvice: Keep movement value if it noticeably improves Anime Apocalypse survival or clear speed.

rows.bestUseCase: First few runs before you unlock better abilities
rows.rerollAdvice: Do not spend too many Anime Apocalypse Trait Rerolls here; save them for stronger abilities.

animeApocalypseCardsGuide.title: Anime Apocalypse Cards Guide for Better Run Choices
animeApocalypseCardsGuide.subtitle: Anime Apocalypse cards appear during a run and shape your temporary build with economy, cooldown, survivability, and drop bonuses.
animeApocalypseCardsGuide.intro: Anime Apocalypse uses a roguelike card layer between waves or after bosses, letting each run develop differently. Economy cards are powerful early, cooldown reduction improves wave-clearing speed once your build is stable, and extra lives become more valuable when survival pressure rises.

items.title: Wealth
items.description: Wealth increases the value of an Anime Apocalypse run by improving resource gain. It is one of the best early picks because faster economy helps you buy upgrades, roll more, and strengthen future attempts.
items.pickTiming: Prioritize Wealth early before your Anime Apocalypse economy is stable.

items.title: Cooldown Reduction
items.description: Cooldown reduction lets you cycle ability skills more often, which directly improves Anime Apocalypse wave clearing when your main ability already has strong damage.
items.pickTiming: Pick cooldown value after you have enough economy or when waves start getting dense.

items.title: Extra Lives
items.description: Extra lives give more room for mistakes during difficult rooms, boss fights, or long Anime Apocalypse Infinite mode runs. They are strongest when enemy density rises and one bad position can end the run.
items.pickTiming: Pick extra lives when Anime Apocalypse survival pressure becomes more important than raw farming speed.

items.title: Drop Chance Increase
items.description: Drop chance increases improve the reward side of an Anime Apocalypse run by helping you collect more useful drops from combat. It fits farming-focused dungeon runs where the goal is stronger progression value.
items.pickTiming: Pick drop chance when your Anime Apocalypse build already clears waves safely.

items.title: Coin Bonus
items.description: Coin-focused cards are useful because coins support upgrades and future rolls. They are especially good in Anime Apocalypse early progression when every run should improve permanent power.
items.pickTiming: Pick coin bonuses early while you still need core Anime Apocalypse upgrades.

items.title: Defensive Card Stack
items.description: Defensive picks become more valuable in Anime Apocalypse Infinite mode because waves keep scaling. Combining survival cards with a strong defensive gadget such as Infinity can make long runs much safer.
items.pickTiming: Pick defensive stacking once enemy pressure starts outpacing your Anime Apocalypse damage.

faq.title: Anime Apocalypse FAQ
faq.subtitle: Answers for Anime Apocalypse codes, abilities, gadgets, maps, and first-run progression.

questions.question: What is Anime Apocalypse on Roblox?
questions.answer: Anime Apocalypse is a Roblox anime zombie roguelike survival game where players clear waves, roll abilities and gadgets, pick cards, farm rewards, and improve their builds.

questions.question: How do Anime Apocalypse codes help new players?
questions.answer: Anime Apocalypse codes give coins, spins, boost timers, raid tickets, and trait rerolls that speed up early ability rolls, gadget rolls, upgrades, and farming sessions.

questions.question: Which Anime Apocalypse ability should beginners use first?
questions.answer: Anime Apocalypse beginners should learn the starter Zorro kit first, then keep a smoother roll such as Coyote, Shadow Monarch, Stark, or another ability that clears waves consistently.

questions.question: How does the Anime Apocalypse Ability Gacha work?
questions.answer: Anime Apocalypse Ability Gacha uses Normal Spins and Lucky Spins, includes Rare through Supreme rarities, and has pity milestones at 50 rolls for Legendary and 100 rolls for Mythic.

questions.question: What maps should Anime Apocalypse beginners start with?
questions.answer: Anime Apocalypse beginners should start with Shibuya for cleaner room flow, then move into Impel Down after learning dash timing, double jump, air dash, and wall movement.

questions.question: What should Anime Apocalypse players upgrade early?
questions.answer: Anime Apocalypse players should prioritize cooldown speed, movement comfort, ability access, gadget support, and economy tools so each run clears rooms faster and survives longer.

biomes.whatIsDescription: Anime Apocalypse maps are anime-inspired Roblox survival areas with room layouts, zombie waves, bosses, traversal routes, and farming opportunities.

whySteps.step1Description: Understand room flow, exits, enemy groups, and movement paths.
whySteps.step2Description: Choose maps that match your ability, gadget, card build, and reward goals.
whySteps.step3Description: Use map space, dash timing, and cooldown windows to stay alive.

building.title: Anime Apocalypse Farming
building.description: Master Anime Apocalypse coins, drops, spins, raid tickets, mastery, battle pass progress, and upgrade priorities.
building.metaTitle: Anime Apocalypse Farming Guide - Coins, Drops and Spins
building.metaDescription: Anime Apocalypse farming guide covering coins, drops, spins, raid tickets, mastery, cards, boosts, daily rewards, quests, and upgrade priorities.
building.whatIsDescription: Anime Apocalypse farming is the process of clearing waves, using cards and boosts, collecting drops, and turning rewards into upgrades.

whySteps.step1Description: Use wave clearing, Wealth cards, codes, quests, and boost timers.
whySteps.step2Description: Run maps and boost windows that support item, ticket, and mastery goals.
whySteps.step3Description: Turn farming rewards into cooldown speed, movement comfort, abilities, gadgets, traits, and cards.

support.title: Anime Apocalypse Support
support.description: Anime Apocalypse support topics covering controls, movement, Roblox access, community links, and basic troubleshooting.
support.metaTitle: Anime Apocalypse Support - Controls and Roblox Help
support.metaDescription: Anime Apocalypse support guide covering controls, movement, Roblox access, Discord links, gameplay basics, and player help topics.
support.whatIsDescription: Anime Apocalypse support content helps Roblox players find controls, community links, movement notes, and basic gameplay answers.

whySteps.step1Description: Review attack, skill, gadget, ultimate, dash, slide, double jump, and wall movement inputs.
whySteps.step2Description: Use the Roblox game page, Roblox group, Discord, and gameplay video links.
whySteps.step3Description: Clarify first-run flow, map selection, cards, upgrades, and reward spending.
